messages.content:
Good afternoon, 
  
On behalf of the National Security and Intelligence Review Agency (Review Agency), we acknowledge receipt of your email sent on December 12, 2025.
Please note that the Registrar communicates with parties and members of the public regarding complaint investigations. 
  
However, the Registrar does not handle inquiries related to reviews under paragraph 8(1)(b) of the _NSIRA Act_. For any inquiries concerning reviews, please contact our general inbox at INFO@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca. 
  
Best regards,
**Registry Officer**
National Security and Intelligence Review Agency Secretariat
registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca | **Tel:** 1-833-890-0293
NSIRA’S X: https://x.com/nsiracanada | NSIRA’s LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/nsira-ossnr/
**Agente du Greffe**
Secrétariat de l’Office de surveillance des activités en matière de sécurité nationale et de renseignement
registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca | **Tél. :** 1-833-890-0293
X de l’OSSNR : https://x.com/OSSNRCanada | LinkedIn de l’OSSNR :https://www.linkedin.com/company/nsira-ossnr/

Si ce message vous a été transmis par erreur, veuillez en informer l'expéditeur et le supprimer immédiatement.
**From:** Yilei Tu (Brunner) <abuzhidaoa8@gmail.com>   
**Sent:** Friday, December 12, 2025 9:37 AM  
**To:** Registrar-Greffier (NSIRA-OSSNR) <registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca>  
**Subject:** Re: NSIRA File 07-403-387 – Update
Dear NSIRA Registry,
Thank you for your recent email and for clarifying the Review Agency’s mandate with respect to complaints under section 16 of the _National Security and Intelligence Review Agency Act_ (NSIRA Act).
I respectfully wish to clarify that my request is **not** for NSIRA to investigate a _public complaint_ against the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) under section 16 of the Act. Rather, I am requesting that the Review Agency consider exercising its **statutory review authority** under **section 8(1)(b)** of the NSIRA Act.
Section 8(1)(b) provides that NSIRA’s mandate includes the authority to:
> _“review any activity carried out by a department that relates to national security or intelligence.”_
CBSA is a federal department, and the security screening activities carried out by CBSA’s Intelligence & Enforcement Branch—specifically through the **Security Screening and Refugee Directorate and the Centre for Immigration National Security Screening (CINSS)**—are clearly national security and intelligence activities within the meaning of section 8(1)(b).
By way of brief context:
*   CSIS completed its security assessment on me and returned my file to CBSA on **June 11, 2025**, as confirmed in CSIS correspondence.
    
*   GCMS/CAIPS notes confirm that CBSA has received the relevant files (including SRR / PILLAROTT entries).
    
*   All recorded security indicators (indictment, extradition, military service, litigation, terrorist screening information) are marked as **“No.”**
    
*   Despite this, CBSA security screening has now been ongoing for **over six months**, with repeated responses indicating only that “work remains ongoing.”
    
On **December 2, 2025**, I received an email from CINSS PUBLIC / CFSNI PUBLIC (CBSA/ASFC) <CINSS_PUBLIC-CFSNI_PUBLIC@cbsa-asfc.gc.ca> confirming that screening remains ongoing, using language identical to prior boilerplate responses received in October and December. This has led to a genuine concern that my file may be subject to an administrative bottleneck rather than substantive national security analysis.
I fully respect the importance of national security screening. However, the prolonged delay has placed me in an extremely difficult position. My PhD program at the University of British Columbia has already been deferred, and continued uncertainty now jeopardizes my academic funding, enrollment, and future career. I am therefore seeking assurance that the national security screening process within CBSA is being carried out appropriately, diligently, and without unnecessary administrative delay.
Given that CSIS has completed its role and is no longer substantively involved, and that the remaining activity is being carried out by CBSA in the context of national security screening, I respectfully submit that this matter falls squarely within NSIRA’s **review mandate under section 8(1)(b)**.
Accordingly, I would be grateful if the Review Agency could confirm whether it is able to consider this matter as a **review of CBSA national security and intelligence activities**, distinct from a complaint investigation under section 16.
Thank you very much for your time and consideration. I appreciate the Review Agency’s continued attention to this file and look forward to your guidance.
Best regards,
Yilei Tu
------------------------
He/Him
PhD Student (Incoming)
Dept of Computer Science, University of British Columbia
Vancouver, BC, Canada
**From:** Registrar-Greffier (NSIRA-OSSNR) <registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca>  
**Date:** Friday, December 12, 2025 at 21:44  
**To:** Yilei Tu (Brunner) <abuzhidaoa8@gmail.com>  
**Cc:** Registrar-Greffier (NSIRA-OSSNR) <registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca>  
**Subject:** RE: NSIRA File 07-403-387 – Update
Good morning,
In accordance with CSIS’s letter dated November 28, 2025, CSIS’s role in the security screening process is completed. Please refer to the Annex attached to the letter for more information on the security screening process. 
Please note that the Review Agency has the legislated mandate to receive and investigate public complaints related to any activity carried out by CSIS pursuant to section 16 of the _National Security and Intelligence Review Agency Act_ (_NSIRA Act_). The Review Agency does not have a mandate to receive or investigate complaints against the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) or Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC).
We ask you to please inform the Review Agency of any updates to your application (including a decision from IRCC to grant or deny the application) when they become available.
Best regards,
**Registry Officer**
National Security and Intelligence Review Agency Secretariat

**From:** Yilei Tu (Brunner) <abuzhidaoa8@gmail.com>  
**Sent:** Tuesday, December 9, 2025 10:36 AM  
**To:** Registrar-Greffier (NSIRA-OSSNR) <registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca>  
**Subject:** Re: NSIRA File 07-403-387 – Update
Dear NSIRA Registry,
Thank you for your recent email.
As of today, IRCC has not issued a final decision regarding my study permit application. CSIS has confirmed that it completed its security assessment on June 11, 2025 and transmitted the results to both IRCC and the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA).
My understanding is that the continuing delay relates to security screening being conducted by CBSA’s Intelligence & Enforcement Branch, specifically within the Security Screening and Refugee Directorate, Centre for Immigration National Security Screening (China and Americas section).
In light of this situation, I would be grateful if the Review Agency could clarify whether NSIRA retains any authority or mechanism to review or oversee national security and intelligence activities conducted by CBSA in this context.
The National Security and Intelligence Review Agency Act, as well as Government of Canada public sources, indicate that NSIRA has the authority to review national security and intelligence activities across the Government of Canada, including those carried out by CBSA. My complaint concerns precisely these national security and intelligence functions within CBSA, rather than other administrative functions.
Could you please confirm whether NSIRA is able to exercise jurisdiction or initiate any form of review at this stage, given that the matter concerns CBSA’s national security screening activities and that CSIS is no longer substantively involved?
I appreciate your clarification and thank you for your continued assistance.
Best regards,
Yilei Tu
NSIRA File Number: 07-403-387
------------------------
He/Him
PhD Student (Incoming)
Dept of Computer Science, University of British Columbia
Vancouver, BC, Canada
https://yileitu.github.io/

**From:** Tu Yilei <abuzhidaoa8@gmail.com>  
**Sent:** Wednesday, December 3, 2025 9:30 PM  
**To:** Registrar-Greffier (NSIRA-OSSNR) <registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca>  
**Subject:** Re: NSIRA File 07-403-387 – Update
Dear NSIRA,
Thank you for following my case. I hereby confirm that IRCC has **NOT** made a final decision on my study permit application. 
I have carefully read the Service’s Letter, it seems that my study permit application is blocked by CBSA right now, as CSIS already finished their job. CBSA has **NOT** confirmed to me whether they have finished their job of security screening. I sincerely hope that CBSA could finish their security assessment and IRCC could make decision on my study permit as soon as possible. 
Thank you again for your help!
Best regards,
Yilei Tu
**From:** Registrar-Greffier (NSIRA-OSSNR) <registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca>  
**Date:** Thursday, December 4, 2025 at 00:41  
**To:** abuzhidaoa8@gmail.com <abuzhidaoa8@gmail.com>  
**Cc:** Registrar-Greffier (NSIRA-OSSNR) <registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca>  
**Subject:** NSIRA File 07-403-387 – Update
Good morning,
Please find enclosed a letter provided to the Review Agency by the Service, dated November 28, 2025.
Please note that the Review Agency is forwarding this letter for your information and is not endorsing its contents. We will conduct our own independent inquiries into the matters raised in the letter.
In light of the Service’s letter, the Review Agency asks you to please confirm whether Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) has made a final decision regarding your immigration application. We ask for your response **by no later than December 8, 2025.**
Best regards,
**Registry Officer**
National Security and Intelligence Review Agency Secretariat

基于我跟NSIRA的邮件往来，给mailto:INFO@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca发邮件，开宗明义Registrar-Greffier (NSIRA-OSSNR) <registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca>让我联系你们的。我希望你们按照8(1)(b) of the _NSIRA Act启动对_**Centre for Immigration National Security Screening (CINSS) of Security Screening and Refugee Directorate of I**ntelligence & Enforcement Branch of CBSA 的调查/review。CINSS一定跟national security or intelligence，所以适用于8(1)(b) 。我的Study Permit申请于4月10日，CSIS于6月11日完成了对我的安调，这两个月的时长我还能接受。但自此以后，我的case就被CINSS，迄今以超过半年了，中间没有一点进展的音信。12月2日CINSS还回复邮件给我说我的case仍然在in progress，我希望你们能监管CINSS的工作效率，这个部门是否在消极怠工尸位素餐？CINSS的国家安全调查的权限远远小于CSIS，CSIS都能两个月完成我的case，为什么CINSS要这么久？请敦促CINSS尽快完成我的case。
  
此外，我已联系过多个渠道投诉CINSS，首先CBSA自己不受理对自己的投诉，其次PSIC也说admin delay不算做 wrongdoing 不受理投诉。public complaints and review commission虽已通过立法但尚未成立未运作。目前加拿大法律里只授权了NSIRA监管CINSS，请担负起责任。我们学签申请者每一天的等待都是无尽的煎熬，我的人生规划完全被这件事打乱了

下面是一封可直接发送到 **INFO@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca** 的英文邮件草稿（语气正式、开宗明义说明 Registrar 让你联系该邮箱，并明确请求 NSIRA 依据 **s. 8(1)(b)** 启动对 **CBSA–CINSS** 的 review）。你可以把方括号内容替换成你的具体信息/附件名称。

---

**Subject:** Request for NSIRA Review under s. 8(1)(b) — CBSA CINSS National Security Screening Delay (Study Permit) / NSIRA File 07-403-387

Dear NSIRA Information Office,  

I am writing further to my correspondence with the NSIRA Registrar (registrar-greffier@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca). In their email of December 12, 2025, the Registrar advised that inquiries relating to reviews under paragraph 8(1)(b) of the *NSIRA Act* should be directed to this general inbox (INFO@nsira-ossnr.gc.ca).  

I respectfully request that NSIRA consider initiating a review under **paragraph 8(1)(b)** of the *National Security and Intelligence Review Agency Act* in relation to national security and intelligence activities carried out by the **Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA)**—specifically the **Centre for Immigration National Security Screening (CINSS)** within the **Security Screening and Refugee Directorate, Intelligence & Enforcement Branch**—in the context of my ongoing security screening for a study permit application.

### Basis for request (NSIRA Act, s. 8(1)(b))
Paragraph 8(1)(b) authorizes NSIRA to “review any activity carried out by a department that relates to national security or intelligence.” CBSA is a federal department, and CINSS’s immigration national security screening functions plainly relate to national security and intelligence. My request is **not** for NSIRA to investigate a public complaint against CBSA under section 16 (which I understand applies to CSIS complaints). Rather, I am asking NSIRA to consider exercising its **statutory review authority** under **s. 8(1)(b)** with respect to CBSA/CINSS national security screening practices and delays.

### Summary of my case and concerns
- I submitted my study permit application on **April 10, 2025**.  
- **CSIS completed its security assessment on June 11, 2025** and returned/transmitted the file to IRCC/CBSA (as confirmed in CSIS correspondence shared with NSIRA).  
- Since CSIS completed its role, my file has remained in **CBSA/CINSS** processing. As of today, the screening has been ongoing for **more than six months** with no substantive progress communicated to me.  
- On **December 2, 2025**, CINSS (CINSS_PUBLIC-CFSNI_PUBLIC@cbsa-asfc.gc.ca) again responded that my screening remains **in progress**, using boilerplate language similar to prior responses.

I fully recognize the importance of national security screening. However, the duration and lack of meaningful updates raise a serious concern that the delay may reflect an administrative bottleneck or inefficiency rather than substantive national security analysis. The impact on me is severe: my UBC PhD program has been deferred and continued delay jeopardizes funding, enrollment, and my academic trajectory.

### Why I am seeking NSIRA review
I have attempted other channels without success:
- CBSA does not accept complaints about its own activities in this context in a way that provides independent oversight.  
- I was advised that other complaint avenues do not accept “administrative delay” matters absent defined wrongdoing.  
- The Public Complaints and Review Commission framework is not yet operational.  

As a result, NSIRA appears to be the only federal body currently empowered to conduct **independent review** of CBSA national security and intelligence activities of this kind.

### What I am asking NSIRA to do
I respectfully ask NSIRA to:
1. **Confirm** whether my request can be considered as a **review matter** under **s. 8(1)(b)** concerning CBSA/CINSS national security screening activities; and  
2. **Consider initiating a review** (or otherwise examining, within NSIRA’s discretion and mandate) whether CINSS is conducting national security screening in a timely and appropriate manner, including whether systemic administrative delays are affecting applicants without justification.

For ease of reference, my previous correspondence with NSIRA is under **NSIRA File No. 07-403-387**. I can provide supporting documents upon request, including: CSIS correspondence confirming completion of its role, relevant GCMS notes, and CINSS email responses.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I would appreciate guidance on the appropriate next steps and any additional information NSIRA requires.

Sincerely,  
**Yilei Tu**  
[UCI / Application number, if you wish]  
[Phone number]  
[Mailing address, optional]  
[Email]

---

如果你愿意，我也可以帮你做两个版本的“力度调整”：
1) **更克制、更法律化**（更像正式的 review request memo）；  
2) **更强硬、更强调人身影响与紧迫性**（但仍保持礼貌专业）。  
你告诉我你更希望哪种风格，以及是否要附上你掌握的证据清单（GCMS条目、CSIS信、CINSS邮件截图等）。
